title: Bridge Paper: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of EEG, fMRI, and fNIRS Studies on the Psychological Impact of Nature on Well-Being.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Ecology/Animism** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Nature connectedness — biophilia hypothesis, nature neuroscience, environmental neuroscience.
**Phase context:** nature connectedness, biophilia, nature neuroscience, environmental neuroscience, nature exposure

Paper: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of EEG, fMRI, and fNIRS Studies on the Psychological Impact of Nature on Well-Being.
DOI: 10.3390/ijerph23030377
Abstract: Exposure to nature has been associated with benefits to human well-being, commonly evaluated using standardized psychological assessments and, more recently, neuroimaging modalities such as Electroencephalography (EEG), functional Magnetic Resonance Imaging (fMRI), and functional Near-Infrared Spectroscopy (fNIRS). This systematic review and meta-analysis addresses the following questions. (1) How is the impact of nature on well-being studied using psychological and neuroimaging modalities and w
